Off Calendar Jun-26-2020 Text Ruling

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify
Off Calendar Jun-26-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Frank Busch appeared by telephone for Stanford Martin, Jr. (individual capacity, and CAV Distributing Corp.)
Lawrence Rockwell appeared by telephone for George Whitney.
Sarah Brooks appeared by telephone for Stanford Martin, Jr. (Trustee).
Edward Thirkell and Julia Lingys appeared by telephone for Stanford Martin, Sr.

Ms. Lingys and Mr. Busch presented oral argument to support their positions with respect to the Motion to Disqualify. The Court informs the parties that the ruling remains the same as the tentative ruling that was e-mailed to the parties. The Motion to Disqualify is denied for the reasons stated in the e-mail. The Court will prepare a form of order.

The Court will also draft an order after status conference adjusting future dates and indicating that all future matters are to be heard in Dept. 305. The Court was informed that the San Mateo Court has indicated that they are short staffed and that they have been unable to process the transfer. Discussion was held as to the scope of the depositions. The Petition for Surcharge and Removal of Trustee is continued from August 18, 2020 to September 11, 2020 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 305 for status of the motion for summary judgment and how to proceed on the surcharge petition.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion To Transfer Related Matter From San Mateo And Consolidate With This Action
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Continued To Jan-31-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 305

Petition For Instructions Regarding Enforceability Of Marital Provisions
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Continued To Jan-14-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 305

Petition For Instructions Regarding Enforceability Of Marital Provisions
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Continued To Jan-31-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 305
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Edward D. Thirkell, Esq. for Stanford Martin, Sr.; Joseph E. Gruber, Jr., Esq. for and with Stanford Martin, Jr. as Trustee; Lawrence K. Rockwell, Esq., for and with George Whitney III; Frank Busch, Esq.,for and with Stanford Martin Jr., in his individual capacity.

Court in session, not reported. Counsel state their appearances. At the Court's invitation, counsel report to the Court on the posture of the case and general issues before the Court, including the various trusts, provisions of the trusts and marital clause of the trust.

Mr. Busch advises the Court that his client is seeking a change of venue for the Family Trust matter currently pending in San Mateo County pursuat to C.C.P. Sec. 403. Mr. Thirkell notes that due to his client's age (93) and health concerns, he will be filing a preference motion. Counsel note that no discovery has been conducted and that discovery will be needed, particularly for Mr. Busch's anticipated motion for summary judgment. Consensus is that there are few percipient witnesses.

Court and counsel generally discuss past mediation in San Mateo before Judge Gallagher and possible mediation in San Francisco.

The Court notes the transfer motion is scheduled for January 29, 2020. After discussion, counsel orally stipulate to continue that hearing to January 31, 2020 at 9:30 a.m. which hearing will also include case management conference. Counsel should be prepared to discuss discovery matters and scheduling of other motions and/or trial dates at that time. If possible, the Court will issue a tentative ruling on the transfer motion but in any event counsel should appear at the Jan. 31 hearing.

Court adjourns.

Judge: Hon. Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D305)

Petition For Instructions Regarding Enforceability Of Marital Provisions
Off Calendar Dec-30-2019 Continued To Jan-14-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ed Thirkell for Stanford Martin, Sr. (Petitioner).
Joseph Gruber for and with Stanford Martin, Jr. (Trustee).
Frank Busch for and with Stanford Martin, Jr. (individual capacity).
Lawrence Rockwell appeared by telephone for and with George Whitney (present in Court).

The Court recused itself from hearing this matter and referred it out to the Department of the Presiding Judge for reassignment. The Petition for Instructions Regarding Enforceability of Marital Provisions is continued to January 14, 2020 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 206.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Trausch. Not reported.
